title: 10 Essential Oils for Soothing Inflamed Gums Naturally

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Catechin is good for inflamed gums because it has strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and fight off bacteria that cause gum inflammation.
Quercetin is good for inflamed gums because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight free radicals that can harm gum tissue.

Tannins are good for inflamed gums because they have strong antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight off bacterial infections in the mouth, promoting healthy gum healing and preventing further inflammation.
Luteolin is good for inflamed gums because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the gums, making it a natural remedy for gum inflammation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil, also known as tea tree oil, has antibacterial properties that help combat the cause of inflamed gums. When plaque and bacteria in the mouth cause gum inflammation, the oil's active compounds like cineole and terpinen-4-ol work to kill these germs. This helps reduce the swelling and redness associated with inflamed gums. The oil also has anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe the gums and ease pain. Additionally, tea tree oil's antiseptic properties help prevent infection and promote healing in the gums. When used as part of a regular oral care routine, it can help reduce gum inflammation and prevent future occurrences.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oil, also known as lemongrass oil, which helps with inflamed gums, you will need fresh lemongrass stalks and a distillation apparatus. First, harvest or purchase fresh lemongrass stalks with long, thin leaves and cut them into small pieces. Next, place the lemongrass pieces into a distillation apparatus, typically a steam distiller, and add water to the apparatus. As the steam rises through the lemongrass, it carries the essential oils out of the plant and into a collection vessel. The mixture of essential oils and water is then cooled, and the essential oils are separated from the water through a process called decantation. The resulting lemongrass essential oil can be used topically, diluted with a carrier oil, to help soothe and reduce inflammation in the gums, while also providing a fresh and citrusy scent.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal essential oils for inflamed gums

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for inflamed gums because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and pain in the gums.

Tannins are good for inflamed gums because they help to reduce swelling and fight bacteria that cause infection, which in turn helps to soothe and heal inflamed gums naturally.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oil is good for inflamed gums because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums. The oil contains a compound called eucalyptol, which is responsible for its healing properties. When applied to the gums, eucalyptol helps to kill bacteria and other germs that can cause inflammation. This can help to prevent infections and promote healing. The oil also has antiseptic properties, which help to prevent the growth of new bacteria. This makes it a useful natural remedy for gum inflammation caused by gum disease, mouth injuries, or poor oral hygiene.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oil that helps with inflamed gums, you'll need fresh lemongrass leaves and a distillation kit. First, harvest the lemongrass leaves when they are in full growth, and make sure they are free of any contamination. Next, chop the lemongrass leaves into small pieces and place them in the distillation kit's steam basket. Then, attach the kit's condenser and collection vessel to the distillation apparatus. Now, heat the water in the distillation kit to produce steam, which will pass through the lemongrass leaves and extract their essential oils. As the steam cools down in the condenser, it will release the extracted essential oils into the collection vessel. Collect the oil and let it settle for a few hours, then decant it into a separate container. This Cymbopogon citratus essential oil can be used to help soothe inflamed gums and promote healthy oral hygiene.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal essential oils for inflamed gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis essential oil, also known as sage oil, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help with inflamed gums. When applied to the gums, it can reduce swelling and ease pain. The oil contains compounds like camphor and salvene, which have antimicrobial effects that prevent the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause infections. This helps to prevent further inflammation and promotes healing. Additionally, sage oil has antiseptic properties, which help to protect the gums from infection and promote healthy tissue growth. Its antioxidant properties also help to neutralize free radicals, which can damage gums and contribute to inflammation. When used in moderation, sage oil can be a natural and effective way to soothe and heal inflamed gums.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oil that helps with inflamed gums, you'll need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. First, you'll need fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves, also known as lemongrass, which you can grow yourself or buy from a local market. Next, you'll need a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, and a small saucepan. To start, combine 1 cup of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves with 2 cups of carrier oil in the saucepan and heat it on low heat for 2-3 hours. This process is called infusion, and it allows the oil to extract the beneficial properties from the lemongrass leaves. After the infusion is complete, let the mixture cool and then strain it through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the oil from the solids. Discard the solids and transfer the Cymbopogon citratus oil to a clean glass bottle. To use this oil for inflamed gums, simply apply a few drops to a cotton swab and gently rub it onto the affected area.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal essential oils for inflamed gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils may help reduce inflamed gums. This is because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe swollen tissues and calm pain. When applied topically, the oil can penetrate deep into the gum tissue, reducing redness and swelling. The anti-inflammatory compounds in the oil may also help reduce bacteria in the mouth, which can contribute to gum inflammation. Echinacea purpurea essential oils may also have antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infection and promote healing in the gums. Some studies suggest that Echinacea purpurea may also have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals. This oil may be used in combination with other natural remedies, such as coconut oil, to help treat inflamed gums.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils that help with inflamed gums, you will need fresh lemongrass leaves and a distillation kit or a steam distiller. First, choose fresh and healthy lemongrass leaves with no signs of wilting or damage. Then, cut the leaves into small pieces and place them in the distillation kit. Next, add water to the distiller and heat it until the water starts to boil. As the steam rises, it will pass through the lemongrass leaves, releasing their natural oils. These oils will then be collected in a separate container. Continue distilling for about 2-3 hours or until the oil collection slows down. Once the distillation process is complete, filter the oil to remove any impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle. This lemongrass essential oil can be used topically to help soothe and calm inflamed gums, but make sure to dilute it with a carrier oil before applying it to the gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oil, also known as peppermint oil, is a popular natural remedy for inflamed gums. This is because peppermint oil has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and ease pain. When applied to the affected area, the menthol in peppermint oil numbs the gums and helps to numb the pain. This cooling sensation also helps to reduce the inflammation, allowing the gums to heal faster. Additionally, peppermint oil has antibacterial properties that help to fight off the bacteria that can cause gum infections. By reducing inflammation and fighting off bacteria, peppermint oil helps to create an environment that allows the gums to heal naturally. Peppermint oil also helps to improve blood circulation, which brings oxygen and nutrients to the affected area, promoting healthy gum tissue growth. This combination of properties makes peppermint oil a useful remedy for inflamed gums.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils for inflamed gums, you will need fresh lemongrass leaves, a carrier oil, a glass jar with a lid, a cheesecloth or coffee filter, and a clean glass bottle. First, harvest fresh lemongrass leaves and wash them thoroughly with clean water to remove any dirt or impurities. Next, dry the lemongrass leaves in a warm, dark place or use a food dehydrator to prevent bacterial growth. Once the leaves are completely dry, chop them into small pieces and add them to a glass jar with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. The general ratio is 1 part dried lemongrass to 3 parts carrier oil. Close the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the oil infuse into the lemongrass. After the infusion period, strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and keeping the infused oil. This herbal essential oil can be used as a mouthwash or added to a toothpaste to help soothe and reduce inflammation of the gums.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for inflamed gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a natural remedy that can help with inflamed gums. The essential oil extracted from ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain. When applied to the gums, ginger oil can help calm the inflammation and prevent further irritation. Ginger's active compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent bacterial growth and infection in the mouth. This can help reduce the risk of conditions like gum disease and bad breath. Regular use of ginger oil can also help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gums, making it a good option for people with inflamed gums.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils for inflamed gums, you will need a few basic ingredients and some equipment. First, gather fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves, also known as lemongrass, and clean them thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris. Next, dry the leaves in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator to remove excess moisture. Once the leaves are completely dry, you can proceed to make the essential oil by using a steam distillation process or solvent extraction. For steam distillation, place the dried leaves in a distillation apparatus and steam them to release the oil, then collect the oil in a separate container. Alternatively, for solvent extraction, steep the dried leaves in a carrier oil like coconut oil for several days, then strain and separate the oil from the leaves. Finally, filter the essential oil to remove any impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle to preserve its properties. You can use this Cymbopogon citratus essential oil to help reduce inflammation and soothe inflamed gums by applying a few drops to a cotton swab and gently massaging it onto the affected area.

remedies.remedy_desc: Syzygium aromaticum, also known as clove oil, is a popular natural remedy for inflamed gums. This essential oil is extracted from the buds of the Syzygium aromaticum tree, which is native to the Maluku Islands in Indonesia. Clove oil contains a compound called eugenol, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. When applied to inflamed gums, clove oil helps to reduce swelling and ease pain. Eugenol in clove oil also fights against bacteria that can cause gum infections, promoting a healthy gum environment. Additionally, clove oil's antiseptic properties help to prevent the growth of new bacteria, keeping the gums protected from further inflammation. By reducing inflammation and fighting bacteria, clove oil provides relief from swollen and painful gums, making it a valuable natural remedy for gum health.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils for inflamed gums, start by gathering fresh lemongrass leaves and stems. You will need a large quantity of these to extract the oil. Next, dry the lemongrass leaves and stems completely by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Once they are completely dry, put the dried lemongrass in a large container with a lid and pour a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil over it. The ratio of dried lemongrass to carrier oil should be about 1 part lemongrass to 3 parts oil. Seal the container and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the container every few days. This process is called maceration and it helps to extract the oil from the lemongrass. After the maceration period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids. You now have a lemongrass oil that can be used to help reduce inflammation in the gums. It can be applied topically to the affected area using a cotton swab or a few drops in water can be used as a mouthwash.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis essential oil, also known as marigold oil, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe inflamed gums. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils that help with inflamed gums, you'll need a few basic materials. First, you need to harvest some fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves, also known as lemongrass, from your garden or a nearby store. Make sure the leaves are clean and free of any dirt or pesticides. Next, you'll need a distillation apparatus, which can be a simple setup of a pot, a condenser, and a collection vessel. Fill the pot with water and add the Cymbopogon citratus leaves, making sure they are completely submerged. Then, heat the water over medium heat, allowing the steam to rise and pass through the leaves. As the steam cools down, it will condense back into a liquid, which is the essential oil. Collect the oil in a separate vessel and let it cool down completely. Once the oil has cooled, you can filter it through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove any impurities. Finally, store the Cymbopogon citratus essential oil in a dark glass bottle and use it as needed to help soothe inflamed gums. Note: Before using this oil, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Commiphora molmol essential oil is a natural remedy for inflamed gums. It has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain. The oil contains compounds like beta-boswellic acid, which blocks the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body. This means that the oil can help stop the inflammation process, allowing the gums to heal faster. The antibacterial properties of Commiphora molmol essential oil also help fight off the bacteria that cause gum infections, such as gingivitis and periodontitis. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, the oil helps soothe and calm the inflamed gums, making it easier to manage symptoms and prevent further damage. Additionally, the oil's antioxidant properties help protect the gums from oxidative stress, which can contribute to inflammation and gum disease.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils that help with inflamed gums, start by gathering the necessary materials: fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, a glass jar, a cheesecloth or coffee filter, and a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid. First, harvest the fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves and gently wash them in cold water to remove any dirt or debris. Next, dry the leaves thoroughly using a clean towel or by air-drying them in a shaded area. Once the leaves are dry, place them in a clean glass jar and cover them with the carrier oil, making sure the leaves are completely submerged. Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it steep for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the oil infuse into the leaves. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a dark glass bottle, discarding the solids. Finally, store the Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oil in a cool, dark place and use it as needed to help soothe and calm inflamed gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus, also known as lemongrass, is a natural remedy for inflamed gums. The essential oil extracted from its leaves has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain. It contains compounds like citral and geraniol, which have antimicrobial effects that fight bacteria and other germs that cause gum infections. When applied to the affected area, the oil's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation, ease swelling, and promote healing. This helps prevent further damage to the gums and surrounding teeth. Regular use of lemongrass essential oil may also reduce plaque and bad breath, promoting a healthier mouth environment. Additionally, its analgesic properties help alleviate pain and discomfort, making it a soothing and natural remedy for inflamed gums.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oil that helps with inflamed gums, start by harvesting fresh lemongrass leaves. Choose healthy leaves with no signs of damage or decay, and clean them thoroughly with water. Next, dry the lemongrass leaves in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator on a low heat setting to prevent burning. Once the leaves are completely dry, they can be crushed or chopped into small pieces. This step is crucial as it releases the essential oils from the lemongrass leaves. Now, place the chopped lemongrass in a clean glass container with a tight-fitting lid, and add a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. The general ratio is 1 part lemongrass to 5 parts carrier oil. Store the container in a cool, dark place and let it steep for 2-3 weeks, shaking the container every few days. After the steeping period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container, and discard the solids. The resulting liquid is your Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oil, which can be used topically to help soothe and reduce inflammation in the gums.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils for inflamed gums

intro_desc: Herbal essential oils can be beneficial for inflamed gums due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, contains compounds like terpinen-4-ol that help reduce inflammation and combat bacteria that can cause gum infections. Eucalyptus globulus oil has decongestant properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums. When gums are inflamed, it can lead to painful chewing, difficulty eating, and even tooth loss if left untreated. Fixing this issue is crucial as it can improve overall oral health and prevent more serious problems. By reducing inflammation and addressing the underlying causes of gum inflammation, you can alleviate symptoms and improve your overall quality of life. Regular use of herbal essential oils can also boost your immune system, reducing the likelihood of future gum infections.
supplementary_best: For inflamed gums, the best combination of herbal essential oils is 'Melaleuca alternifolia' (tea tree oil), 'Eucalyptus globulus' (eucalyptus oil), and 'Mentha x piperita' (peppermint oil). 'Melaleuca alternifolia' has antibacterial properties that help fight the infection causing the inflammation. 'Eucalyptus globulus' reduces inflammation and relieves pain with its anti-inflammatory properties. 'Mentha x piperita' cools and numbs the gums, providing instant relief from pain and discomfort. This combination works well together because 'Melaleuca alternifolia' and 'Eucalyptus globulus' target the infection and inflammation, while 'Mentha x piperita' provides quick relief from pain.
supplementary_avoid: If you have inflamed gums, it's best to avoid certain herbal essential oils that can irritate or inflame the condition further. Cinnamon oil (Cinnamomum verum) is one such oil to avoid. It has a strong chemical compound called cinnamaldehyde, which can cause the gums to become more inflamed and sensitive. Another oil to be cautious with is Thyme oil (Thymus vulgaris). Although thyme has antibacterial properties, its high concentration of thymol can be harsh on inflamed gums, potentially causing more discomfort and pain. Additionally, Clove oil (Eugenia caryophyllata) should be used with caution as it contains eugenol, which can further irritate the gums and exacerbate inflammation. Finally, Oregano oil (Origanum vulgare) contains carvacrol, a compound that can be too potent for inflamed gums, potentially leading to increased sensitivity and discomfort.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera gel is a natural remedy that can help with inflamed gums. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease pain. Aloe vera contains a compound called acemannan, which has been shown to have antimicrobial properties that can fight off bacteria that can cause gum infections. When applied to the gums, aloe vera gel can help to soothe and calm the area, making it a popular choice for people who want a natural remedy to help manage gum inflammation.  Turmeric, a spice commonly used in cooking, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and ease pain in inflamed gums. Curcumin, the active compound in turmeric, has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing. When consumed as a supplement or added to food, turmeric can help to reduce inflammation in the gums and promote healthy gum tissue.  Tea tree oil may not be the only essential oil that can be used to help with inflamed gums, but it is often associated with it. However, a lesser known oil is clove oil which has antiseptic properties that can help to fight off bacteria that can cause gum infections. Clove oil has been used for centuries to help to reduce pain and inflammation in the mouth, and can be applied topically to the gums to help to soothe and calm the area.  Saltwater mouthwash is another natural remedy that can help with inflamed gums. Salt has antimicrobial properties that can help to kill bacteria that can cause gum infections, and can also help to reduce swelling and ease pain. When mixed with warm water and swished around the mouth, saltwater mouthwash can help to soothe and calm the gums, making it a popular choice for people who want a natural remedy to help manage gum inflammation.  Gargling with warm water can also help to reduce inflammation in the gums. When done regularly, it can help to loosen and remove plaque and bacteria that can cause gum infections, and can also help to reduce swelling and ease pain. This can be especially helpful for people who have a hard time accessing a dentist or for those who want to supplement their regular oral hygiene routine with a natural remedy.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with inflamed gums often experience other health issues related to poor oral health and immune system problems. One common condition is heart disease, as bacteria from inflamed gums can enter the bloodstream and cause inflammation in the heart. Medicinal herbs like turmeric and ginger have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in both the gums and the heart.  Another issue is diabetes, as inflamed gums can indicate insulin resistance and poor blood sugar control. Herbs like berberine and cinnamon have been shown to improve insulin sensitivity and lower blood sugar levels, which can help manage diabetes symptoms.  Inflamed gums are also linked to digestive problems, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). The gut and mouth are connected through the gut-brain axis, and inflammation in the gums can lead to inflammation in the gut. Herbs like slippery elm and marshmallow root have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help calm digestive issues.  Additionally, people with inflamed gums may experience fatigue, headaches, and joint pain due to chronic inflammation. Herbs like ashwagandha and willow bark have anti-inflammatory and adaptogenic properties that can help reduce inflammation and promote overall well-being.  Herbs like tea tree oil and oregano oil have antimicrobial properties that can help kill bacteria and reduce inflammation in the gums, while also supporting immune function. These herbs can be used topically or taken internally to promote healing and reduce symptoms.
